mainEntity.name: My workers comp claim for a fall was denied because they say it’s a pre-existing condition. What now?

acceptedAnswer.text: A denial isn't the end. You can appeal by proving the workplace fall "aggravated" or "accelerated" your condition. In Utah and Idaho, even if you had a prior injury, you are eligible if the work incident made it worse. Seeking a legal appeal is the next step.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Insurance companies often deny slip and fall claims by arguing the injury was "pre-existing" or "idiopathic" (meaning it just happened internally). To win an appeal in Idaho or Utah, you must provide "medical causation" evidence. This usually requires a doctor's statement clarifying that the fall was the primary cause of your current symptoms or that it significantly worsened a quiet, non-symptomatic condition.

The appeal process involves a formal hearing before an Administrative Law Judge. 

Success depends on consistent medical records, credible testimony, and often a second medical opinion from a favorable doctor.

Do not give up if you receive a denial letter. You have a limited time to file an Application for Hearing. Collect all your medical records and seek a legal review to identify why the carrier is or is not accepting the claim. An attorney is almost always necessary to navigate the formal litigation of a denial.
